page: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz, equipped with a 320 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 1024.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it bundles four NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs with a total of 384 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided via 7600 GB of NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 400 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple domains. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, as well as in ray tracing and image processing tests. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark CPU scores also rank in the top tier. For database and web operations, the instance delivers top-tier static web serving throughput and Passmark database operations, alongside strong redis SET operations. LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models consistently rank in the top tier.

Given the high density of premium hardware, including four Blackwell GPUs, one terabyte of DDR5 memory, and 7.6 TB of local NVMe SSD, this instance represents a high-resource tier designed for demanding workloads. The qualitative cost efficiency is aligned with its massive resource density, trading a higher baseline cost for extreme parallel processing and memory capacity. It is highly suited for large-scale LLM inference, graphics-intensive rendering, ray tracing, high-throughput database operations, and complex data science tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge is a high-performance, graphics-intensive server built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C vCPUs, 1024.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and four NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs delivering 384 GB of total VRAM. Local storage is supported by 7600 GB of NVMe SSD, complemented by a 400 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in Geekbench multi-core, ray tracing, and image processing, alongside top-tier LLM inference speeds for models up to 70B parameters. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier. This server is optimized for GPU-accelerated workloads, large-scale LLM inference, ray tracing, and high-throughput database applications requiring substantial memory and local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR5 RAM, four NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs with 384 GB VRAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge features 96 Intel vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR5 RAM, 4 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD for GPU-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU server combining four Blackwell accelerators, one terabyte of DDR5 memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: Equipped with 4 NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs providing 384 GB of total VRAM.
Features 96 dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C vCPUs and 1024 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory.
Includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 400 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers top-tier benchmark performance in LLM inference, ray tracing, and database operations.
Suited for large-scale machine learning, graphics rendering, and data-intensive applications.
